Title: Genome assembly of wild tea tree DASZ reveals pedigree and selection history of tea varieties

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 176 diverse tea accessions / DASZ-based association panel
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive tea phenolic contributing to flavor and health benefits
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with variation of gallic acid content in tea leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. W10g022427 was located in an mQTL for GA content in third leaves. 2. It was annotated as a HXXXD-type acyltransferase-like protein. 3. The gene was predicted to catalyze modification of p-coumaroyl-CoA and shikimate to chlorogenic acid, linking phenylpropanoid metabolism to GA-associated variation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The catalytic role was predicted, not directly biochemically validated in this paper; relationship to GA is association-based.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown accessions sampled on the same day in Wuhan, China
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly, RNA-seq (PRJNA595795), GWAS/mQTL metabolite profiling, genome sequencing PRJNA595851, resequencing PRJNA595898
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for selecting GA-associated haplotypes, but requires direct functional validation before deployment.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate genes were selected partly based on correlation between phenotype and gene expression, but gene-specific pattern not reported

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation branch leading to trihydroxylated flavonoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for catechin monomers and is associated with ECG accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid substrates such as naringenin, eriodictyol, dihydrokaempferol, and dihydroquercetin to form 3′,4′- and/or 3′,4′,5′-hydroxylated products including dihydromyricetin
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: DASZ reference genome / tea population alleles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major bioactive catechin contributing to tea astringency and human health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allelic variation affects catechin/flavonoid metabolite profiles and favorable allele is associated with higher ECG content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/mQTL association; genotype-phenotype analysis; transient overexpression in tobacco; purified protein incubation with tea extracts; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. W12g026224/CsF3′5′H showed a strong association with ECG content in young and mature leaves. 2. Four nonsynonymous SNPs formed genotypes, with T/CGTT associated with high ECG. 3. Two contrasting alleles altered metabolite profiles in transient tobacco assays and in vitro tea extract assays, confirming allelic functional divergence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper links the gene to ECG variation, while mechanistically it supplies hydroxylated precursors for multiple catechin monomers.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 217 accessions (PRJNA595795), metabolite profiling/GWAS, genome assembly PRJNA595851
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme; key control point not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient overexpression in tobacco; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown tea leaves; transient expression in tobacco leaves; in vitro incubation with tea extract
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favorable alleles/haplotypes can be used for marker-assisted selection or metabolic engineering to enhance catechin production, especially ECG-related profiles.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms terminal flavan-3-ol monomers and modulates catechin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to flavan-3-ol monomers required for proanthocyanidin/condensed tannin formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Flavan-3-ol monomers such as epicatechin-related products
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: DASZ reference genome / tea population alleles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major tea polyphenols contributing to astringency and health-promoting activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allelic variation affects catechin content; favorable genotype CGT is associated with higher catechin levels in young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/mQTL association; genotype-phenotype analysis; transient overexpression in tobacco; purified enzyme incubation with tea extracts; enzyme kinetics with purified proteins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. W08g018649/CsANR was associated with catechin content in young leaves. 2. Three nonsynonymous SNPs were significantly associated with catechins, and genotype CGT conferred higher levels than GCC-related genotypes. 3. Functional assays showed both alleles altered flavonoid metabolites, and enzyme kinetics demonstrated CsANRa had much higher Km and lower catalytic efficiency than CsANRb, supporting allelic functional divergence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The lower catalytic efficiency of CsANRa may partially block cyanidin biosynthesis and thereby favor catechin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 217 accessions (PRJNA595795), metabolite profiling/GWAS, genome assembly PRJNA595851
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Late-pathway control step; key control point supported by allelic effects but not explicitly designated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient overexpression in tobacco; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown tea leaves; transient expression in tobacco leaves; in vitro incubation with tea extract
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for marker-assisted breeding or allele engineering to increase catechin accumulation; allelic kinetics should be considered when designing editing strategies.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms anthocyanidin intermediate upstream of catechin/epicatechin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins via leucoanthocyanidin dioxygenase activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive catechin contributing to tea flavor and health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with epicatechin content variation in mature leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. W13g027058/ANS was associated with epicatechin content in mature leaves. 2. The gene encodes anthocyanidin synthase with leucoanthocyanidin dioxygenase activity. 3. Its position in the late flavonoid pathway supports its causal link to EC variation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Association-based candidate only; no direct functional validation reported in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 217 accessions (PRJNA595795), metabolite profiling/GWAS, genome assembly PRJNA595851
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown accessions sampled on the same day
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a marker candidate for EC-associated alleles, pending functional validation.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: DASZ reference genome / tea population alleles
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive catechin contributing to tea flavor and health-promoting properties
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator associated with EC variation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allelic variation affects flavonoid metabolite profiles and higher EC content is associated with the CA/TA genotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/mQTL association; genotype-phenotype analysis; transient overexpression in tobacco with metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. W12g025045/CsMYB5 was significantly associated with EC content in third leaves. 2. Three nonsynonymous SNPs formed alleles, and CA/TA genotype was associated with higher EC levels. 3. Transient overexpression of contrasting alleles in tobacco altered secondary metabolite profiles, supporting allelic regulatory divergence.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as ortholog of Arabidopsis MYB5, previously implicated in proanthocyanidin formation; direct downstream targets were not mapped in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 217 accessions (PRJNA595795), metabolite profiling/GWAS, genome assembly PRJNA595851
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient overexpression in tobacco; promoter not specified
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown tea leaves; transient expression in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory allele for breeding or engineering enhanced catechin composition; should be combined with structural genes for stronger metabolic output.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: DASZ reference genome / tea population alleles
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major tea catechin with health-promoting and flavor-related properties
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate MYB regulator associated with ECG variation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allelic variation is associated with higher ECG content in mature leaves; A/TC/A genotype showed the highest ECG levels
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. W13g026373/CsTT2 was associated with ECG content in mature leaves. 2. Two nonsynonymous SNPs showed significant association, and A/TC/A genotype conferred higher ECG levels. 3. The favorable genotype was detected only in pop1, indicating subpopulation-specific enrichment.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotated as ortholog of Arabidopsis TT2, known for proanthocyanidin regulation, but this tea gene was not functionally validated in tobacco or direct target assays in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 217 accessions (PRJNA595795), metabolite profiling/GWAS, genome assembly PRJNA595851
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown accessions sampled on the same day
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory haplotype for selecting high-ECG germplasm; best applied with structural gene markers due to lack of direct mechanistic validation.
